Question:
My opponent controls a "Lyrilusc - Recital Starling", which has gained the effect "[this card's] control cannot switch" due to "Lyrilusc - Beryl Canary"'s effect.

If my opponent now activates the effect of this "Lyrilusc - Recital Starling", can I activate the effect of "Number F0: Utopic Draco Future" in response? If yes, what happens when it resolves?
Official_Answer:
Even in this situation, the effect of "Number F0: Utopic Draco Future" can be activated.

When it resolves, the activation of "Lyrilusc - Recital Starling"'s effect will be negated, but control of it will not change. ("Lyrilusc - Recital Starling" will remain on the opponent's field.)

Question:
In a situation where my "Topologic Bomber Dragon" is in the Extra Monster Zone, a "Honeybot" and a "RAM Clouder" were Special Summoned from my Graveyard with the『Target any number of monsters in your Graveyard; Special Summon them, and if you do, you lose 1000 Life Points for each monster Special Summoned by this effect』effect of "Soul Charge".

At this time, if "RAM Clouder" is Special Summoned in a Main Monster Zone that "Honeybot" points to, will the『If another monster is Special Summoned to a zone a Link Monster points to, while this monster is on the field: Destroy all monsters in the Main Monster Zones, also your other monsters cannot attack for the rest of this turn』effect of "Topologic Bomber Dragon" activate?
Official_Answer:
『A monster being Special Summoned to a zone a Link Monster points to』refers to a situation where a monster is Special Summoned to a zone a Link Monster that's already in the Monster Zone points to. Therefore, unless "Honeybot" or "RAM Clouder" is Special Summoned to a zone that "Topologic Bomber Dragon" or another Link Monster that's already in the Monster Zone, points to, the effect of "Topologic Bomber Dragon" won't activate.

This is the same for the following cards, unless a monster is Special Summoned to a zone a Link Monster that's already in the Monster Zone points to, even if multiple monsters, including Link Monsters, are Special Summoned at the same time so that they would be linked, their effect cannot be activated:
● "Sea Archiver"
● "Tindangle Base Gardna"
● "Topologic Zeroboros"
● "Topologic Blaster Dragon"

Question: When activating the『equip this card to that target』effect of "Graydle Alligator", can I target a "Botanical Lion" whose『possession of this face-up card on the field cannot change』effect is applying?
Official_Answer:
Yes, you can.
(You can activate "Graydle Alligator"'s effect by targeting a monster, even if possession of it cannot change.)

However, you will not be able to gain control of your opponent's "Botanical Lion". Regardless, "Graydle Alligator"'s『when this card leaves the field, destroy the equipped monster』effect will still apply.

Additionally, if "Botanical Lion"'s effects become negated due to some other effect after "Graydle Alligator" has been equipped to it, "Graydle Alligator"'s effect will cause you to gain control of "Botanical Lion".

The same also applies to the following monsters, whose effects prevent their possession from being changed:
● "Heart of the Blue-Eyes"
● "Element Valkyrie"
● "Element Magician"
● "Element Soldier"
● "Horus the Black Flame Dragon LV4"
● "Mataza the Zapper"
● "Botanical Lion"
● "Blindly Loyal Goblin"
● A Synchro Monster Summoned using "Taotie, Shadow of the Yang Zing" as material
● An Xyz Monster Summoned using "Lyrilusc - Beryl Canary" as material

This also applies to the following cards/effects, which can be activated by targeting a monster even if control of it cannot change:
● "Masked Beast Des Gardius"
● "Graydle Cobra"
● "Graydle Eagle"
● "Dark Necrofear"
● "Old Entity Hastorr"'s ①st effect
● "Heretical Phobos Covos"'s ①st effect
● "Sky Striker Mecha - Widow Anchor"
● "Mikanko Reflection Rondo"
● "Battle Teleportation"

Question: The『reduce the next battle damage you would take this turn to 0』effect of "Performage Damage Juggler" is already applying. In this situation, can I activate the『During the Battle Phase (Quick Effect): You can discard this card; reduce the next battle damage you would take this turn to 0.』effect of a second copy of "Performage Damage Juggler"?
Official_Answer:
Even if that effect of "Performage Damage Juggler" has already resolved previously this turn, and even if the『reduce the next battle damage you would take this turn to 0』effect has not yet been applied to reduce the battle damage, that same effect of another "Performage Damage Juggler" can still be activated.

However, if you then proceed to take battle damage, all of those『reduce the next battle damage you would take this turn to 0』are consumed. (You will not reduce multiple instances of battle damage to zero.)

Question: An "Infernoble Knight Emperor Charles", which is treated as an Equip Spell, is face-up in my Spell & Trap Zone. If I now activate the ③rd effect of "Noble Arms Museum", will it prevent me from Special Summoning monsters for the rest of this turn, except Warrior monsters?
Official_Answer:
That effect of "Noble Arms Museum" will not be applied to you.

Even if "Infernoble Knight Emperor Charles" is face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone, you still control a card named ""Infernoble Knight Emperor Charles"".

Question: Can the Pendulum Effect of "Wisdom-Eye Magician" be activated while the effect of "Anti-Spell Fragrance", or of "Diabellze the Original Sinkeeper", is being applied?
Official_Answer:
Yes, you can. (If you do, it will resolve normally.)
Placing a Pendulum Monster in a Pendulum Zone by the Pendulum Effect of "Wisdom-Eye Magician" is not treated as activating a Spell Card.

Question: While "Final Attack Orders" is applying its effect, my opponent Special Summons a monster in Defense Position. Due to "Final Attack Orders"' effect, that monster is immediately changed to Attack Position. Will the effect of a "Slifer the Sky Dragon" on the field now activate?
Official_Answer: Since your opponent Special Summoned the monster in Defense Position, "Slifer the Sky Dragon"'s effect will not activate.

Question:
While a face-up "Performapal Sword Fish" is in my Monster Zone, I perform multiple Special Summons during resolution of a Chain.

In this case, how will "Performapal Sword Fish"'s effect be activated after the Chain has resolved?
Official_Answer: After the Chain has finished resolving, the effect of "Performapal Sword Fish" will activate exactly once, regardless of how many Special Summons you performed during that Chain.

Question:
When I activate the ②nd or ③rd effect of "Walls of the Imperial Tomb" whose name is treated as "King's Sarcophagus", I chain the『You can shuffle 1 of your monsters that is banished or in your Graveyard into the Deck, or if that monster mentions that Field Spell, you can Special Summon it instead』effect of "Krishnerd Witch".

Can I then Special Summon "Horus the Black Flame Deity" that mentions "King's Sarcophagus" in its text instead of return into the Deck?
Official_Answer:
No, you cannot. (Similarly, you also cannot Special Summon "Imsety, Glory of Horus", "Qebehsenuef, Protection of Horus", "Duamutef, Blessing of Horus", or "Hapi, Guidance of Horus".)

""King's Sarcophagus"" is the name of a Continuous Spell, not the name of a Field Spell.

Question: When resolving "Numbers Eveil", can I choose "Number F0: Utopic Future" or "Number XX: Utopic Dark Infinity" as either the "Number" monster to Special Summon from my Extra Deck, or as one of the "Number" monsters to attach to the Summoned monster?
Official_Answer:
"Number F0: Utopic Future" can be chosen as one of the monsters to be attached. (Its Rank will be treated as 1.)

"Number XX: Utopic Dark Infinity" cannot be chosen when resolving the effect of "Numbers Eveil".

Question: The effect of my opponent's "Droll & Lock Bird" has previously resolved this turn, and I cannot add cards from my Deck to my hand as a result. Can I activate the ①st effect of a "Danger! Bigfoot!" in my hand?
Official_Answer:
Yes, you can.

When it resolves, your opponent still chooses a random card from your hand, and you still discard that card. If the discarded card was not a ""Danger! Bigfoot!"", you then also still Special Summon a "Danger! Bigfoot!" from your hand. The effect's resolution then ends without you drawing a card from your Deck.

This also applies to the other "Danger!" monsters' effects: "Danger! Nessie!", "Danger! Chupacabra!", "Danger!? Jackalope?", "Danger! Thunderbird!", "Danger! Dogman!", "Danger! Mothman!", "Danger!? Tsuchinoko?", and "Danger! Ogopogo!".

Question: During the turn that I activated the effect of "Ancient Gear Wyvern", can I activate "Ancient Gear Drill"?
Official_Answer: The effect of "Ancient Gear Wyvern" prevents you from Setting cards for the rest of this turn. The effect of "Ancient Gear Drill" Sets a Spell Card directly from your Deck, so in this scenario you cannot activate it. (Also note that since you cannot Set cards, "Ancient Gear Drill" itself cannot be Set from your hand to the Spell & Trap Zone.)

Question:
When the opponent activates "ZERO-MAX", can I chain the ①st effect of "Stardust Dragon"?

Also, when the opponent activates "ZERO-MAX", can I chain the ②nd effect of "Shooting Star Dragon", the ②nd effect of "Ruddy Rose Dragon", the ②nd monster effect of "Enneacraft - Archa.TAIL", "My Body as a Shield", "Musakani Magatama", or "Destruction Jammer"?
Official_Answer:
When "ZERO-MAX" is activated, if there's a monster face-up in the Monster Zone with less ATK than the monster selected as the target, the effect of "ZERO-MAX" would destroy a monster(s) on the field, so the effect of a card such as "Stardust Dragon" can be chained to it.

Also, when "ZERO-MAX" is activated, if there isn't any monster face-up in the Monster Zone with less ATK than the monster selected as the target, the effect of "ZERO-MAX" would not destroy a monster(s) on the field, so the effect of a card such as "Stardust Dragon" cannot be chained to it.

This is the same when the opponent activates "Hero Blast", as long as there's a monster face-up in your Monster Zone with ATK less than or equal to the monster selected as the target, you can activate the ①st effect of "Stardust Dragon", the ②nd effect of "Shooting Star Dragon", the ②nd effect of "Ruddy Rose Dragon", the ②nd monster effect of "Enneacraft - Archa.TAIL", "My Body as a Shield", "Musakani Magatama", or "Destruction Jammer".

Question: In a situation where an opponent's monster that was Special Summoned from the Extra Deck has changed control with the ①st effect of "D/D/D Headhunt", if that monster is destroyed, can the『If a face-up "D/D/D" and/or "Dark Contract" card(s) you control, except ""D/D/D Zero Doom Queen Machinex"", is destroyed while this card is face-up in your Extra Deck, even during the Damage Step: You can Special Summon this card, then you can destroy 1 card on the field』monster effect of "D/D/D Zero Doom Queen Machinex" be activated?
Official_Answer:
It can be activated.
(This is because the card's name while on the field is referenced.)

This is the same for the activation conditions of the following cards' effects:
● "Malefic Truth Dragon"
● "Performage Plushfire"

Question: If your opponent's "Bujingi Wolf" that you currently control is banished with "Dark Core", which player can activate the effect of "Bujin Arasuda" in the hand to Special Summons itself?
Official_Answer: In the given situation, you, the player that controlled "Bujingi Wolf" when it was banished, not your opponent, its original owner, can activate the effect of "Bujin Arasuda" in the hand to Special Summon itself.

Question:
Due to its own effect "Aiwass, the Magistus Spell Spirit" is equipped to an opponent's monster and I took control of it.

In that situation, if "Aiwass, the Magistus Spell Spirit" that is treated as an Equip Card is destroyed, who will be the controller of the previously equipped monster?
Official_Answer: If "Aiwass, the Magistus Spell Spirit" that is treated as an Equip Card is destroyed, the control of the equipped monster will be returned immediately, and the effect that prevents that monster from activating its effects will no longer apply.

Question:
When performing the resolution of "Evenly Matched" activated by the opponent, I chose a "Floowandereeze & Robina" that's face-up on my field as the card to banish.

In this case, what happens to "Floowandereeze & Robina"?
Official_Answer:
It will be banished face-up.

For example, this is the same for the effects of the following cards that cause a card to be『banished if it would leave the field』, if banished due to "Evenly Matched" being activated, it will be banished face-up:
● "Floowandereeze & Eglen"
● "Floowandereeze & Stri"
● "Floowandereeze & Toccan"
● "Exodius the Ultimate Forbidden Lord"
● "Dark Magician of Chaos"
● "Core of Chaos"
● "Shell of Chaos"
● "Cockadoodledoo"
● "Plaguespreader Zombie"
● "Battle Fader"
● "Quillbolt Hedgehog"
● "Snopios, Shade of the Ghoti"

Similarly, if a card such as "Floowandereeze & Robina" would be banished face-down due to the effects of the following cards, it will be banished face-up:
● "Kashtira Fenrir"
● "Kashtira Arise-Heart"
● "PSY-Frame Overload"

Question: If I Tribute "Zoma the Spirit", or a monster Token, for the Tribute Summon of "The Tyrant Neptune", what happens to "The Tyrant Neptune"'s ATK/DEF?
Official_Answer:
"The Tyrant Neptune"'s ATK/DEF will be increased by the ATK/DEF printed on the Tributed monsters.
Therefore, if "Zoma the Spirit" (ATK 1800/DEF 500) is Tributed, "The Tyrant Neptune" will gain 1800 ATK and 500 DEF.
If a "Rose Token" (ATK 800/DEF 800) Special Summoned by "Black Garden"'s effect is Tributed, "The Tyrant Neptune" will gain 800 ATK and 800 DEF.
If an "Emissary of Darkness Token" (ATK ?/DEF ?) Special Summoned by "Gorz the Emissary of Darkness"' effect is Tributed, "The Tyrant Neptune" will not gain any ATK or DEF.
